title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dry eyes
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dry eyes are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can alleviate the symptoms of dry eyes. Examples of such herbs include chamomile, calendula, and eyebright. These tinctures can help stimulate tear production, reduce redness and inflammation, and protect the eyes from environmental irritants. By incorporating herbal tinctures into their eye care routine, individuals can experience relief from dry eye symptoms and improvements in their overall eye health and comfort. This can lead to a better quality of life, as they no longer have to deal with the discomfort and vision problems caused by dry eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tincture helps with dry eyes because it contains a variety of beneficial compounds, such as tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes. The herb has astringent properties that can help to tighten and tone the tissues around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness. Additionally, eyebright tincture can help to stimulate the production of tears, providing lubrication and moisture to dry eyes. Its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties can also help to protect the eyes from damage caused by environmental factors, such as pollution and dryness. Overall, eyebright tincture can provide a natural and effective way to soothe and relieve dry eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms, due to its active constituents like alkaloids, tannins, and flavonoids.
Astringent: The astringent properties of eyebright tincture help to tighten and strengthen eye tissues, reducing redness and irritation caused by dry eyes, due to its high tannin content.
Antimicrobial: Eyebright tincture inhibits the growth of microorganisms that can exacerbate dry eye symptoms, thanks to its active compounds like phenolic acids and essential oils.
Demulcent: Eyebright tincture forms a protective film over the eyes, providing soothing relief for dry eye symptoms, due to its mucilaginous polysaccharides.
Analgesic: Eyebright tincture helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with dry eyes by reducing inflammation and numbing the affected area, due to its alkaloids and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Eyebright herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 5 ounces of vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use 1-2 drops in each eye as needed for dry eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in active compounds, the flowers are used in tinctures for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Leaves: High in therapeutic constituents, the leaves are often used in tinctures to soothe and moisturize dry, irritated eyes.
Seeds: Packed with beneficial compounds, the seeds can be used in tinctures to help alleviate dry eye symptoms and support overall eye health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with dry eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe irritation and reduce inflammation in the eyes. The herb's mucilaginous quality can also help to lubricate and moisturize the eyes, providing relief from dryness and discomfort. Additionally, plantain tincture has been shown to have astringent properties that can help to tighten and tone the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. By promoting healthy eye tissue and providing essential nutrients, plantain tincture can help to alleviate the symptoms of dry eyes and support overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eyes caused by inflammation. Active constituents include allantoin, flavonoids, and mucilage.
Demulcent: Plantain tincture forms a soothing film over the eyes, helping to retain moisture and alleviate dry eye symptoms. This is due to the mucilage content in plantain.
Astringent: Plantain tincture tightens and tones the eye tissue, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry eyes. The tannins in plantain are responsible for this astringent effect.

Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms, helping to prevent eye infections that can exacerbate dry eye symptoms. Active constituents include allantoin, aucubin, and flavonoids.

Vitamin-rich: Plantain tincture contains vitamins A, C, and E, which are essential for eye health and can help improve dry eye symptoms. Vitamin A, in particular, plays a crucial role in maintaining a healthy tear film.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a clean, pesticide-free area, rinse, and dry thoroughly.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried plantain leaves into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the plantain leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle. Use 20-30 drops in water, 2-3 times a day, as needed for dry eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and known to reduce inflammation, plantain leaves can help soothe dry and irritated eyes.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and mucilage, plantain seeds can provide lubrication and help alleviate symptoms of dry eyes.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids and tannins, plantain flowers can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rue tincture helps with dry eyes because it contains high levels of alkaloids, which can stimulate the production of tears and reduce eye inflammation. This herbal remedy has been used for centuries to treat various eye conditions, including dry eye syndrome. Rue tincture works by promoting the flow of blood and oxygen to the eyes, which can help to soothe and moisturize dry, irritated eyes. Additionally, the antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of rue tincture can help to protect the eyes from infection and inflammation, making it an effective natural treatment for dry eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rue tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituents like arborinine and coumarins, helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms.
Antihistaminic: The antihistaminic property of rue tincture, owed to its compound rutin, aids in alleviating allergic reactions that may contribute to dry eye symptoms, providing comfort to the eyes.
Astringent: Rue tincture's astringent property, derived from tannins, helps tighten and strengthen the eye tissues, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry eyes.

Analgesic: The analgesic property of rue tincture, due to its active constituents like methyl nonyl ketone and azulene, helps alleviate pain and discomfort caused by dry eyes, providing soothing relief.

Immune-boosting: Rue tincture's immune-boosting property, attributed to its vitamin C content, strengthens the body's immune system, promoting faster recovery and preventing further eye irritations that may worsen dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rue herbs, filling a clean jar halfway with the leaves and flowers, then chop coarsely.
Add 80-proof vodka to the jar, covering the herbs by at least 1 inch and sealing tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the resulting rue tincture in a glass dropper bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark location. For dry eyes, apply 1-2 drops in each eye, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and essential oils, the leaves of the rue plant are often used in tinctures for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Flowers: Containing high levels of antioxidants, rue flowers are used in medicinal tinctures for dry eyes because they can help reduce redness and irritation.
Seeds: With a high concentration of essential oils, rue seeds are used in tinctures for dry eyes as they can help stimulate tear production and provide relief from symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can alleviate the symptoms of dry eye syndrome. The herb contains compounds called flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the eye, thereby reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, fennel is known to stimulate the production of tears and lubricate the eyes, providing relief from dryness and irritation. Its antioxidant properties can also protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dry eye symptoms. Overall, fennel tincture can help soothe and moisturize dry eyes, providing much-needed relief for those who suffer from this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and irritation in dry eyes, thanks to compounds like flavonoids and anethole.
Mucolytic: Fennel tincture's mucolytic properties help break down and clear mucus buildup in the eyes, providing relief for dry eye symptoms, due to the presence of essential oils and fenchone.
Antimicrobial: Fennel tincture's antimicrobial properties help protect the eyes from infection, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms, due to the presence of trans-anethole and limonene.
Analgesic: Fennel tincture's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with dry eyes, thanks to compounds like anethole and estragole.

Antioxidant: Fennel tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of dry eye symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried fennel seeds and place them in a clean jar, filling it up to ¾ with 80-proof vodka or rum.
Tightly seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the recommended time, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle, using a funnel if necessary, and label it with the name, date, and dosage.
Take 30-60 drops of the fennel tincture, up to three times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional, for dry eye relief.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for dry eyes due to their high oil content and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant have a soothing effect on the eyes and can help alleviate dry eye symptoms.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain bioflavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, making them ideal for dry eye tinctures.
Stems: The stems of the fennel plant contain essential oils that can help moisturize and soothe dry, irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Fennel rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, making them a popular choice for dry eye tinctures.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with dry eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe irritation and reduce inflammation in the eyes. Chamomile contains active compounds such as azulene and bisabolol, which can help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling in the eyes. Additionally, chamomile has been shown to have a calming effect on the eyes, which can help to alleviate the discomfort associated with dry eyes. The tincture form of chamomile allows for quick absorption into the body, making it a convenient and effective option for treating dry eyes. Furthermore, chamomile is a natural and gentle herb, making it a safe and suitable option for people who may be sensitive to conventional eye drops or other treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituent chamazulene, helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief for dry eye symptoms.
Astringent: The astringent property of German chamomile, caused by its tannin content, can help tighten and strengthen eye tissues, offering temporary relief for dry eyes.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of German chamomile, attributed to its azulene content, can help protect the eyes from potential infections, which can worsen dry eye symptoms.
Analgesic: German chamomile tincture's analgesic property, owing to its chamazulene and bisabolol content, helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with dry eyes.

Immune-boosting: The immune-boosting property of German chamomile, due to its flavonoid content, can help strengthen the body's overall immune response, promoting faster healing and recovery from dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the desired steeping time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent flowers.
Transfer the liquid tincture to a dark glass dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place; it will last for several years.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and soothing properties, providing relief for dry and irritated eyes.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, chamomile leaves contribute to the overall effectiveness of the tincture, supporting the health of the eyes and reducing inflammation.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and fatty acids, chamomile seeds enhance the tincture's ability to moisturize and protect the eyes, providing long-lasting relief for dry eye symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with dry eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce redness and irritation in the eyes. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and tannins, can help to tighten and tone the tissues around the eyes, thereby reducing dryness and discomfort. Additionally, yarrow has been shown to stimulate the production of tears, providing natural lubrication to the eyes and promoting overall eye health. Its antimicrobial properties can also help to protect the eyes from infection, making it a valuable natural remedy for dry eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids, helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye irritation.
Astringent: The astringent property of yarrow tincture, owing to tannins, helps tighten and strengthen eye tissues, reducing redness and dryness.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture's antimicrobial property, attributed to lactones and essential oils, helps combat eye infections that can contribute to dry eye symptoms.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of yarrow tincture, from alkaloids and volatile oils, helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with dry eyes.

Circulation-boosting: Yarrow tincture's ability to improve circulation, thanks to lactones and essential oils, ensures proper nutrient supply to the eyes, promoting eye health and reducing dry eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, rinse, and let dry for 24 hours.
Chop the dried yarrow into small pieces and place in a clean jar, filling it 3/4 full.
Pour 100-proof vodka into the jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged, and seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle. Use 1-2 droppersful in a small amount of water, up to three times daily.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing dry eye symptoms.
Leaves: Contain high levels of flavonoids and tannins, which can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry eyes.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, particularly omega-3 and omega-6, that can help alleviate dry eye symptoms and promote eye health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with dry eyes because it contains high levels of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can cause dryness and discomfort. Additionally, pot marigold tincture contains flavonoids and carotenoids, which can help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. This herbal remedy also helps to stimulate the production of tears, providing lubrication and moisture to the eyes. Overall, pot marigold tincture can help to alleviate dry eyes, providing relief and comfort to those who suffer from this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help prevent eye infections, which can exacerbate dry eye symptoms, thanks to the active constituents like lutein and zeaxanthin.
Emollient: Pot marigold tincture has a soothing effect on dry, irritated eyes, as it helps to lubricate and protect the eye surface, owing to its rich oil content.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tincture protect the eyes from free radical damage, reducing the oxidative stress that contributes to dry eye symptoms, due to the high levels of flavonoids and carotenoids.

Immune-boosting: By strengthening the immune system, pot marigold tincture helps the body fight off infections and reduce inflammation, contributing to overall eye health and alleviating dry eye symptoms, thanks to the active constituents like vitamin C and E.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried pot marigold flowers and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 100 proof vodka over the flowers until they are completely submerged, then seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the desired steeping time, strain the mixture through cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid.
Store the finished tincture in a dark glass bottle, discarding the spent flowers. Use 20-30 drops in water, up to 3 times daily.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, making them effective in reducing inflammation and providing relief for dry eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them beneficial for soothing and moisturizing dry eyes.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve the quality of tears, providing relief for dry eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tincture helps with dry eyes because it contains berberine, an alkaloid known for its anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and boost tear production, providing relief for dry eye symptoms. Additionally, goldenseal has natural astringent properties that can help to tighten and tone the mucous membranes in the eyes, further alleviating dryness and discomfort. Its antibacterial and antifungal properties can also help to protect the eyes from infection, making it a holistic option for those seeking natural relief for dry eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Goldenseal tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms, due to the presence of alkaloids such as berberine.
Antimicrobial: Goldenseal tincture has the ability to fight off bacterial infections that can contribute to dry eyes, thanks to the isoquinoline alkaloids like berberine and hydrastine.
Astringent: Goldenseal tincture can help tighten and tone eye tissues, reducing redness and dryness, due to the tannins found in the plant.
Immune-boosting: Goldenseal tincture supports the immune system, which can help the body combat dry eye issues, as a result of the isoquinoline alkaloids, particularly berberine.

Improves mucous membrane health: Goldenseal tincture promotes the health of mucous membranes, including those in the eyes, aiding in the production of tears and reducing dry eye symptoms, due to the presence of isoquinoline alkaloids and other active compounds.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried goldenseal root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the goldenseal, ensuring the herb is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the solid goldenseal material.
Store the resulting goldenseal tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. For dry eyes, use 5-10 drops in each eye as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of goldenseal are often used in medicinal tinctures for dry eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to reduce redness and irritation.

Roots: The roots of goldenseal contain berberine, an alkaloid known for its antibacterial and immune-boosting properties, making it useful for treating dry eyes caused by bacterial infections or a weakened immune system.
Seeds: Goldenseal seeds are rich in essential oils and have been used in tinctures to help stimulate the production of tears and relieve symptoms of dry eyes.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of goldenseal contain several beneficial compounds, including berberine and hydrastine, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making them useful for treating dry eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with dry eyes because it contains high levels of rosmarinic acid and allantoin, which have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. These compounds can help to reduce inflammation in the eyes, relieve dryness, and promote the production of tears. Additionally, comfrey tincture can help to soothe and protect the delicate tissue of the eyes, providing relief from irritation and discomfort. Its natural healing properties make it a safe and effective alternative to over-the-counter eye drops for those suffering from dry eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eyes caused by inflammation of the eyelids or eye surface. This property is due to the presence of allantoin and rosmarinic acid.
Wound healing: Comfrey tincture promotes wound healing, which can help with dry eyes caused by damaged or irritated eye tissues. The allantoin and tannins in comfrey contribute to this property.

Pain relief: Comfrey tincture has analgesic properties that help alleviate pain associated with dry eyes, making it an effective natural remedy for discomfort. The presence of rosmarinic acid and mucilage gives comfrey its pain-relieving properties.

Astringent: Comfrey tincture has astringent properties that help tighten and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing tear evaporation and providing relief from dry eyes. The tannins in comfrey are responsible for its astringent effect.

Demulcent: Comfrey tincture acts as a demulcent, forming a soothing film over the eyes and helping to retain moisture. This property is attributed to the mucilage content in comfrey, making it an effective remedy for dry eyes.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried comfrey root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the comfrey, ensuring all pieces are covered, and seal the jar.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the finished comfrey tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Comfrey leaves are rich in mucilage, making them effective in soothing and moisturizing dry, irritated eyes.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers contain anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce redness and inflammation associated with dry eyes.
Rhyzomes: Comfrey rhyzomes have a high concentration of allantoin, which promotes tissue regeneration and aids in healing dry eye symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sumac tincture helps with dry eyes because it contains high levels of tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to reduce eye inflammation and irritation. The tincture can also help to stimulate the production of tears, providing natural lubrication to the eyes and alleviating dryness. Additionally, sumac has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors. By using sumac tincture, individuals can experience relief from dry eyes and improve their overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sumac tincture can reduce inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from dry eye symptoms. This property is important because dry eye syndrome is often associated with inflammation of the eyelid and eye surface. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Astringent: Sumac tincture has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and irritation. This property is important for dry eyes as it can help protect the delicate skin around the eyes from further irritation and damage. The astringent properties come from the tannins present in sumac.

Antioxidant: Sumac tincture contains high levels of antioxidants that can help protect the eyes from free radical damage. This property is important for dry eyes as oxidative stress can contribute to the development and progression of dry eye syndrome. The antioxidant properties of sumac tincture come from its high concentration of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Antimicrobial: Sumac tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent eye infections. This property is important for dry eyes because people with dry eye syndrome may be more susceptible to eye infections due to decreased tear production and increased eye irritation. The antimicrobial properties of sumac tincture are attributed to its high concentration of tannins and essential oils.

Analgesic: Sumac tincture has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate the discomfort associated with dry eyes. This property is important for dry eyes as people with dry eye syndrome often experience symptoms such as burning, stinging, and grittiness in the eyes. The active constituents responsible for sumac's analgesic properties include tannins, flavonoids, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ripe sumac berries, measuring 2 cups, and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 100 proof vodka, exactly 2 cups, over the berries until fully submerged.
Secure a lid on the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out the liquid.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and recommended dosage of 20-30 drops in water, up to 3 times daily.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Sumac leaves are often used in medicinal tinctures for dry eyes due to their high tannin content, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation.
Berries: Sumac berries are rich in antioxidants and can help to soothe dry, irritated eyes by reducing inflammation and providing hydration.

Bark: The bark of the sumac plant contains a variety of beneficial compounds, including tannins and essential oils, which can help to relieve dry eye symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting moisture retention.

Roots: Sumac roots contain a variety of beneficial compounds, including tannins and flavonoids, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote moisture retention in the eyes, making them useful in medicinal tinctures for dry eyes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry eyes with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of calendula, chamomile, and fennel. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to reduce eye irritation and produce tears. Mix equal parts of these tinctures, add a few drops to a cup of water, and use as an eyewash up to three times a day. Alternatively, you can take the tinctures orally, following the recommended dosage on the label. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures include blepharitis and meibomian gland dysfunction. Blepharitis is an inflammation of the eyelids, often caused by bacteria or skin conditions, and can result in symptoms like dry eye. Herbal tinctures such as goldenseal and calendula can be used to reduce inflammation and fight infection. Meibomian gland dysfunction, which can also lead to dry eye symptoms, occurs when the glands that produce the oily layer of the tear film become blocked or inflamed. Warm compresses and massages can help unclog the glands, and herbal tinctures like eyebright and fennel can reduce inflammation and promote healthy tear production. As always, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen.
